details.title: Restored Sony WH-1000XM5 Wireless Industry Leading Noise Canceling Headphones, Silver (Refurbished)
details.description:
• Industry-leading noise cancellation optimized to you
  • Magnificent Sound, engineered to perfection
  • Crystal clear hands-free calling
  • Up to 30-hour battery life with quick charging (3 min charge for 3 hours of playback)
  •  Ultra-comfortable, lightweight design with soft fit leather
  • Multipoint connection allows you to quickly switch between devices
  •  Redesigned collapsible case made for easy storage in a bag
  • WH-1000XM5 headphones feature intuitive touch control settings
  • For everyday convenience, just Speak-to-Chat and Quick Attention mode stop your music and let in ambient sound
  • Instant pause. Instant play

all_reviews.reviewText: I love these. They're so comfortable, great sound quality, and noise canceling is top notch. I love these new colors and shape, comparable to beats and apple but for much less. Just excellent value. The buttons take some getting used to, but after rereading the instructions I've gotten them figured out. I've worn them on a 4 hour flight and was comfortable the entire time. I like the pass through sound, being able to toggle back and forth is great. They're easy to connect between my work laptop, my phone, and my personal devices. Great sound quality of my voice when on the phone or on zoom calls, no tin sound quality or anything, no echo.
all_reviews.itemName: Restored Sony WH-1000XM5 Wireless Industry Leading Noise Canceling Headphones, Silver (Refurbished)

all_reviews.reviewText: I got these headphones less than a year ago and the hinges have snapped out of place on both sides of the headphones. Initially i glued the hinge back into place the first time it happened and now it had happened on the other side. Extremely upset as I paid full price and anytime i speak with anyone about repairs I am told that it does not fall under the warranty. I recommend you save yourself the time and money and invest in other headphones by other companies because this design flaw appears to be common with Sony headphones. I originally enjoyed the sound quality of the project and was certain that the headphones were the ones for me but now that this has happened to both sides and I find it it has happened to many others it makes me question my overall purchase
all_reviews.itemName: Restored Sony WH-1000XM5 Wireless Industry Leading Noise Canceling Headphones, Silver (Refurbished)

clientResponses.response:
Hi Crow92,
Thank you for reaching out to us. We're sorry to hear the issue that you are having with the left ear side of the headphones. This is definitely not the experience we want you to have. Allow us to help troubleshoot the problem. Some devices that emit ultrasonic sound waves designed to repel rodents may occasionally be installed at the entrances of commercial facilities or subway stations. Near such devices, the ultrasonic sound waves may cause noise or unusual sound to be heard from the headset. Leave the place where the noise or unusual sound is heard. Or turn off the noise canceling function/Ambient Sound Mode from the “Sony | Headphones Connect” app. Please refer to this help guide link to troubleshoot if you hear unusual sound or noise from the headphones: https://helpguide.sony.net/mdr/wh1000xm5/v1/en/contents/TP1000534519.html

May we contact you to help and get more information? If so, please email the below information to socialsupport@am.sony.com:

all_reviews.reviewText: My first real pair of headphones. I bought them for ANC, and I am not disappointed. The implementation of ambient and anc mode switching automatically is surprisingly useful. I didn't think I'd use it, but it's good. My burps activate speak to talk and my soft speech isn't always picked up, but it's still quite good. It works well with my PC with a Bluetooth adapter. Ultimately I hope they last me a long time.

clientResponses.response:
Hi Stanash,

Thanks for sharing your review and experience with the WH1000XM5. We're glad that you are enjoying these headphones for phone calls. Your feedback about the noise canceling function and beeping sound is greatly appreciated. For optimum performance, make sure that the software version of the headphones is up-to-date (via the Sony Headphones Connect app). 

Make sure that you are wearing the headphones properly. The noise canceling function is effective in low frequency ranges such as airplanes, trains, offices, near air-conditioning, and is not as effective for higher frequencies, such as human voices. You may also find these links helpful

https://helpguide.sony.net/mdr/wh1000xm5/v1/en/contents/TP1000534745.html
https://helpguide.sony.net/mdr/wh1000xm5/v1/en/contents/TP1000539062.html

If the microphone on the headphones is covered with your hands, the effect of the Noise Canceling feature, Ambient Sound Mode, or Speak-to-Chat mode may not work properly, or a beeping sound (feedback) may occur. If any of these occur, remove your hands from the microphones.

The headphones are equipped with a function to detect a beeping sound (feedback) that will cancel the sound for a short period of time. In rare cases, the Noise Canceling feature, Ambient Sound Mode, or Speak-to-Chat mode may temporarily stop in reaction to a high note such as an alarm or an electronic sound (feedback), but the Noise Canceling function or Ambient Sound Mode will automatically return in about a second. This is not a malfunction.

Hope this helps. In case you need further assistance, please email us at socialsupport@am.sony.com with the following details:

Name:
Username:
Model Number:
Issue:
Telephone Number:

Or contact our product support team via phone call or chat through this link:
https://us.esupport.sony.com/support/s/support-contact?language=en_US&category=headphones 

Sincerely,
-Ralph

clientResponses.response:
Hi Kunal_P,

Thank you for purchasing the WH-1000XM5 Wireless Noise Canceling Headphones. It's unfortunate to learn the noise cancellation hasn't performed to your standards. Allow us to help you and turn your experience around. If the effect of noise canceling is not sufficient, try to adjust the headset into a comfortable position. Check that the Adaptive Sound Control is turned off. When the Adaptive Sound Control is turned on, the noise canceling function may become insufficient.

Because of the Auto NC Optimizer feature (optimizing the Noise Canceling function automatically), the Noise Canceling effect may change depending on the conditions or how you are wearing the headphones.

For example, the following may cause the Noise Canceling effect to change:

• Going down the stairs, eating, drinking, or speaking loudly
• Touching the headphones
• The headphones move or slip slightly

You may refer to this help guide link for troubleshooting steps to isolate and fix this issue: https://helpguide.sony.net/mdr/wh1000xm5/v1/en/contents/TP1000534745.html

Despite your concerns, we're happy to hear that you like the exceptional comfort and excellent audio quality of our Sony Headphones and the WH-1000XM5 is a no exception. Good to know you're utilizing the built-in equalizer on the Sony Headphones Connect app. Thank you for choosing Sony! -Jess

all_reviews.reviewText:
Pros:
1) very light and I can barely feel them
2) sublime sound via analog 3.5mm cord
3) sound cancellation system works pretty well
4) genious buttonless control sensors built in the right headphone
5) good quality of voice recording while speaking on the phone

Downsides:
1) sound quality sucks when connected via bluetooth
2) sound is distorted BADLY when there are any microwaves around (when I warmed up some food, my music was randomly sped up for the time when the microwave oven was working) and probably some other active BT devices around
3) the control sensors doesn't work when connected via analog, they play click sounds when I touch the pad, but nothing happens
4) noise cancellation system created "kick" sounds when I was travelling in a car at high speed with windows open
5) covers are not deep enough, my ears sometimes hit against the speaker when I open my jaw, which creates a distracting rubbing sound

All in all I am pretty satisfied since I got what I was looking for: decent headphones with REALLY decent sound I haven't heard for years which are able to cut me off from pretty much any other sound of the reality around. I was expecting for some low grade behaviour from BT cos they always suck in general, so no surprise there. Sensor control pad is great but must be reworked to be functional at all times. Ear covers could be enlarged too by 20-30%. The rest is as usual - a bit overpriced, a bit glitchy, but does the required job - the Sony style.
all_reviews.itemName: Restored Sony WH-1000XM5 Wireless Industry Leading Noise Canceling Headphones, Silver (Refurbished)

clientResponses.response:
Hi Stanash,

Thanks for sharing your review and experience with the WH1000XM5. We're glad that you are enjoying these headphones for phone calls. Your feedback about the noise canceling function and beeping sound is greatly appreciated. For optimum performance, make sure that the software version of the headphones is up-to-date (via the Sony Headphones Connect app). 

Make sure that you are wearing the headphones properly. The noise canceling function is effective in low frequency ranges such as airplanes, trains, offices, near air-conditioning, and is not as effective for higher frequencies, such as human voices. You may also find these links helpful

https://helpguide.sony.net/mdr/wh1000xm5/v1/en/contents/TP1000534745.html
https://helpguide.sony.net/mdr/wh1000xm5/v1/en/contents/TP1000539062.html

If the microphone on the headphones is covered with your hands, the effect of the Noise Canceling feature, Ambient Sound Mode, or Speak-to-Chat mode may not work properly, or a beeping sound (feedback) may occur. If any of these occur, remove your hands from the microphones.

The headphones are equipped with a function to detect a beeping sound (feedback) that will cancel the sound for a short period of time. In rare cases, the Noise Canceling feature, Ambient Sound Mode, or Speak-to-Chat mode may temporarily stop in reaction to a high note such as an alarm or an electronic sound (feedback), but the Noise Canceling function or Ambient Sound Mode will automatically return in about a second. This is not a malfunction.

Hope this helps. In case you need further assistance, please email us at socialsupport@am.sony.com with the following details:

Hi Kunal_P,

Thank you for purchasing the WH-1000XM5 Wireless Noise Canceling Headphones. It's unfortunate to learn the noise cancellation hasn't performed to your standards. Allow us to help you and turn your experience around. If the effect of noise canceling is not sufficient, try to adjust the headset into a comfortable position. Check that the Adaptive Sound Control is turned off. When the Adaptive Sound Control is turned on, the noise canceling function may become insufficient.

Because of the Auto NC Optimizer feature (optimizing the Noise Canceling function automatically), the Noise Canceling effect may change depending on the conditions or how you are wearing the headphones.

For example, the following may cause the Noise Canceling effect to change:

• Going down the stairs, eating, drinking, or speaking loudly
• Touching the headphones
• The headphones move or slip slightly

You may refer to this help guide link for troubleshooting steps to isolate and fix this issue: https://helpguide.sony.net/mdr/wh1000xm5/v1/en/contents/TP1000534745.html

positive_reviews.reviewText:
Pros:
1) very light and I can barely feel them
2) sublime sound via analog 3.5mm cord
3) sound cancellation system works pretty well
4) genious buttonless control sensors built in the right headphone
5) good quality of voice recording while speaking on the phone

Downsides:
1) sound quality sucks when connected via bluetooth
2) sound is distorted BADLY when there are any microwaves around (when I warmed up some food, my music was randomly sped up for the time when the microwave oven was working) and probably some other active BT devices around
3) the control sensors doesn't work when connected via analog, they play click sounds when I touch the pad, but nothing happens
4) noise cancellation system created "kick" sounds when I was travelling in a car at high speed with windows open
5) covers are not deep enough, my ears sometimes hit against the speaker when I open my jaw, which creates a distracting rubbing sound

All in all I am pretty satisfied since I got what I was looking for: decent headphones with REALLY decent sound I haven't heard for years which are able to cut me off from pretty much any other sound of the reality around. I was expecting for some low grade behaviour from BT cos they always suck in general, so no surprise there. Sensor control pad is great but must be reworked to be functional at all times. Ear covers could be enlarged too by 20-30%. The rest is as usual - a bit overpriced, a bit glitchy, but does the required job - the Sony style.
